title: androcalva incilis
intro: Androcalva incilis, also known as the Tasmanian pea, is a flowering plant native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as acting as an antispasmodic and antiseptic. Androcalva incilis is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, with some herbalists recommending it as a remedy for digestive issues and skin conditions.
